title: Minimum Cyclic Substring with K Swaps and Merges

statement.markdown: Given a string and two integers K and M, find the length of the smallest substring which becomes a palindrome by at most K swaps and at most M merges between any two characters in the substring. A merge operation means changing a pair of characters to the same character.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains the string S. The second line contains the integer K and the integer M space-separated.
statement.outputFormat: The first line contains the minimum length of substring which satisfies the given conditions.
statement.notes: You can consider that the string is a circular string. The swap and merge operations are allowed at most K and M times respectively.

hints.text: Use suffix tree for efficient string matching.

editorial.title: Dynamic Programming with Bit Masking
editorial.intuition: Start with the whole string, then gradually shrink the window size.
editorial.bruteForce: Compare each substring with its reverse, check if it's a palindrome, and count the number of swaps and merges needed.
editorial.optimalSolution: Maintain a bit mask of allowed operations for each substring length.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int find(string s, int k, int m); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution {public int find(String s, int k, int m); };

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int find(string s, int k, int m) { int ans = 0; for (int i = 0; i < s.size(); i++) { if (s[i] != s[i+1]) { ans++; } } return ans; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution {public int find(String s, int k, int m) { int ans = 0; for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) { if (s.charAt(i) != s.charAt(i+1)) { ans++; } } return ans; } };
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(s, k, m) { let ans = 0; for (let i = 0; i < s.length - 1; i++) { if (s[i] != s[i+1]) { ans++; } } return ans; }

functionSignature.cpp: int find(string s, int k, int m);
functionSignature.java: int find(String s, int k, int m);
functionSignature.python: def find(self, s, k, m) -> int:

testCases.input:
First line string, second line k and m separated by space (stdin match)
abacabad
3 2
